generatedFrom: @carbon/charts-react — hand-authored chart catalog from carbon-design-system/carbon-charts source + docs (charts.carbondesignsystem.com). All 25 chart components.

packages.@carbon/charts: Framework-agnostic core (D3-based) + plain styles. Peer dep: d3.
packages.@carbon/charts-react: React wrapper (PRIMARY). One component per chart type.

setup.ssr: Charts need `window`; in Next.js App Router mark the file 'use client' (or dynamic import with { ssr:false }). They are client components.
setup.note: Each chart is `<XChart data={data} options={options} />`. `data` is a flat tabular array; `options` configures axes/theme/legend. Charts re-render on prop change.

dataModel: Carbon Charts use a FLAT TABULAR data array — one row per datum, not nested series. Common fields: `group` (the series/category, drives color + legend), `key` (the x-category for bar/labels), `value` (the measure), `date` (for time series). `options.axes.{left,bottom,right,top}.mapsTo` names which field an axis reads; `scaleType` is 'labels' | 'linear' | 'time' | 'log'. Color is assigned per `group`. Hierarchy/flow/geo charts use specialized shapes (children[], source/target, geo ids) — see each chart's dataShape.

optionsReference.title: string — chart title (sentence case).
optionsReference.height: string e.g. '400px' — chart fills its container width; set an explicit height.
optionsReference.axes: { left|bottom|right|top: { mapsTo: <dataField>, scaleType?: 'labels'|'linear'|'time'|'log', title?: string, includeZero?: boolean, ticks?: {...} } }
optionsReference.color: { scale: { '<group>': '#hex' }, pairing: { option: 1-4 }, gradient?: {...} } — map series to Carbon categorical palette colors; prefer leaving default for accessible palettes.
optionsReference.legend: { enabled?: boolean, position?: 'top'|'bottom'|'left'|'right', alignment?: 'center'|'left'|'right', clickable?: boolean, truncation?: {...} }
optionsReference.tooltip: { enabled?, valueFormatter?(v,label), groupLabel?, showTotal?, customHTML?, alwaysShowRulerTooltip? }
optionsReference.zoomBar: { top: { enabled: true } } — brushable zoom for time series.
optionsReference.data: { loading?: boolean, groupMapsTo?: 'group', selectedGroups?: [] } — set loading:true to show the skeleton state.
optionsReference.donut: { center: { number?: number, label?: string }, alignment?: 'center' }
optionsReference.gauge: { type?: 'full'|'semi', status?: 'danger'|'warning'|'success', showPercentageSymbol?: boolean, deltaArrow?: {...}, numberFormatter?(n) }
optionsReference.meter: { peak?: number, proportional?: {...}, status?: { ranges: [{ range:[min,max], status:'success'|'warning'|'danger' }] } }
optionsReference.points: { radius?, filled?, enabled? } — for line/scatter point marks.
optionsReference.radar: { axes: { angle: <field>, value: <field> }, alignment?, xLabelPadding?, yTicks? }
optionsReference.geoData: Choropleth: a topojson `Topology` passed via `options.geoData` (required). Each datum's `name` is matched against the feature's `properties.NAME`. There is no `thematic.mapsTo` — the color reads each datum's `value` automatically.
optionsReference.accessibility: Built in: every chart ships a keyboard-navigable, screen-reader data view; patterns/redundant encodings for color-vision deficiency; tabular alternative. Always set a meaningful `title` and axis `title`s.

families.intent: compare discrete categories or items side by side
show change/trend across a continuous time axis
show composition / how parts sum to a whole
relationship between measures or spread of values
one headline number, progress to a target, or status
nested structure or flow between stages/nodes
combine multiple chart types on shared axes

charts.name: SimpleBarChart
charts.intent: compare a single measure across discrete categories
charts.avoidWhen: Many categories (>~12 → consider horizontal/lollipop) or part-to-whole (→ stacked/donut).

charts.name: GroupedBarChart
charts.intent: compare multiple series across the same categories, bars side by side
charts.avoidWhen: Composition/totals matter more than per-series values (→ stacked).

charts.name: StackedBarChart
charts.intent: show composition within each category and totals across categories
charts.when: Part-to-whole per category, or totals comparison.
charts.avoidWhen: Precise per-segment comparison across categories (→ grouped/line).

charts.name: LineChart
charts.intent: trend of one or more series over a continuous (usually time) axis
charts.dataShape: [{ group, date, value }]  // or { group, key, value } for categorical x
charts.when: Continuous change; comparing trends of a few series.
charts.avoidWhen: Few discrete categories (→ bar) or part-to-whole (→ area/stacked).

charts.name: AreaChart
charts.intent: trend over time with emphasis on magnitude/volume under the line
charts.when: A single series' magnitude over time.
charts.avoidWhen: Many overlapping series (→ line, or stacked-area for composition).

charts.name: StackedAreaChart
charts.intent: composition over time — how series stack into a total trend

charts.name: ScatterChart
charts.intent: relationship/correlation between two measures, points per datum
charts.dataShape: [{ group, key, value }]  // categorical x; or two linear axes for numeric x/y

charts.name: BubbleChart
charts.intent: scatter with a third measure encoded as bubble size
charts.dataShape: [{ group, key, value, surface }]  // surface drives radius

charts.name: HistogramChart
charts.intent: distribution of a continuous variable via auto-binned counts
charts.dataShape: [{ <numericField>, group }]  // chart bins the numeric field

charts.name: BoxplotChart
charts.intent: distribution summary (quartiles, median, outliers) per category
charts.dataShape: [{ group, key, value }]  // many rows per key; chart computes quartiles

charts.name: HeatmapChart
charts.intent: value intensity across two categorical dimensions (color = magnitude)

charts.name: PieChart
charts.intent: composition of a whole across a few categories
charts.when: ≤ ~5 slices summing to a meaningful whole.
charts.avoidWhen: Many slices or precise comparison (→ bar/donut).

charts.name: DonutChart
charts.intent: part-to-whole with a headline total in the center
charts.avoidWhen: Many slices or precise comparison (→ bar).

charts.name: GaugeChart
charts.intent: progress toward 100% / a target with status and delta
charts.when: One KPI as % of a target, with up/down delta.

charts.name: MeterChart
charts.intent: single value against a peak/target as a thin horizontal meter
charts.dataShape: [{ group, value }]  // one or more rows; peak/status in options
charts.when: Compact KPI vs target (great in cards/tiles).
charts.avoidWhen: Need % of a circular target (→ gauge).

charts.name: BulletChart
charts.intent: KPI vs target with qualitative ranges (compact dashboard metric)
charts.dataShape: [{ title, ranges:[..], marker, value, group }]  // see docs for exact fields
charts.when: KPI vs target with good/ok/bad bands.
charts.docNote: Bullet has a specialized datum (title/ranges/marker/value). Confirm exact fields at the docs site before shipping.

charts.name: LollipopChart
charts.intent: ranked categorical comparison with a lighter ink-to-data ratio than bars
charts.when: Ranked categories where bars feel heavy.

charts.name: RadarChart
charts.intent: compare several series across multiple shared metrics (axes)

charts.name: ComboChart
charts.intent: overlay multiple chart types (e.g. bars + line) on shared axes
charts.dataShape: [{ group, key, value }]  // assign each group to a chart type via comboChartTypes
charts.when: Two related measures with different visual encodings.

charts.name: TreemapChart
charts.intent: hierarchical part-to-whole via nested area-proportional rectangles
charts.dataShape: [{ name, children: [{ name, value }] }]  // nested hierarchy
charts.when: Hierarchy where leaf magnitude matters and space is tight.

charts.name: CirclePackChart
charts.intent: hierarchical grouping via nested circles
charts.dataShape: [{ name, children: [{ name, value }] }]  // nested hierarchy

charts.name: TreeChart
charts.intent: node-link hierarchy (tree or dendrogram)
charts.dataShape: { name, children: [{ name, children: [...] }] }  // single root object

charts.name: AlluvialChart
charts.intent: flow/quantity moving between stages or categories

charts.name: ChoroplethChart
charts.intent: encode a value per geographic region by fill color
charts.dataShape: [{ name, value }]  // `name` must match the topology feature properties.NAME; needs options.geoData (topojson)
charts.docNote: Requires a topojson `options.geoData` (Topology). Each datum's `name` is matched to the feature's `properties.NAME`. There is no `thematic.mapsTo` — color reads `value` automatically. See the docs site for the geoData wiring.

charts.name: WordCloudChart
charts.intent: term prominence by frequency (font size encodes value)
charts.when: Quick qualitative sense of frequent terms.
